full_prompt: Design a Composite Overwrapped Pressure Vessel (COPV) for use as the high-pressure nitrogen pressurant tank in a student-developed bipropellant rocket targeting the 30,000 ft apogee category. The COPV consists of a seamless 6061-T6 aluminum liner (monolithic spin-formed, no welds) overwrapped with a T700-carbon-fiber / epoxy matrix filament winding. Liner internal diameter 150 mm, internal cylindrical length 300 mm, hemispherical domes, single 1/4-inch NPT port on the forward dome. Service fluid gaseous nitrogen at MEOP 300 bar (30 MPa) at 25 C. Composite wrap: [90 / +-15 / 90 / +-15 / 90] layup with T700S carbon/epoxy, ply thickness 0.25 mm, E1 = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, G12 = 5 GPa, nu12 = 0.30, longitudinal tensile strength 2550 MPa, transverse tensile strength 50 MPa. Liner material 6061-T6 (E = 68.9 GPa, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa). Qualification rules: (R1) burst pressure >= 3.0 x MEOP = 900 bar, first-ply-failure criterion (Tsai-Wu) must not exceed 1.0 below 900 bar; (R2) proof pressure of 1.5 x MEOP = 450 bar shall be held for >= 4 minutes (>= 2x nominal hold duration of 120 s), with liner max von Mises <= yield / 1.1 = 251 MPa; (R3) autofrettage cycle at 1.25 x MEOP to pre-compress the liner is permitted; final liner residual stress after autofrettage-and-bleed must be compressive in the hoop direction; (R4) dry mass of fully wrapped COPV <= 3.0 kg; (R5) axial hard-mount fundamental frequency >= 200 Hz. Primary verification is structural FEA with shell-based composite (layered shell or solid-shell) for the wrap and solid elements for the liner; hydrostatic proof and burst simulated as internal pressure with autofrettage as a load-step sequence. Vibration analysis (modal) is secondary for R5. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly (liner + wrap), laminate layup specification, and a report listing dry mass in kg, Tsai-Wu max index at burst, liner max von Mises at proof in MPa, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a Composite Overwrapped Pressure Vessel (COPV) for the high-pressure nitrogen pressurant tank of a student bipropellant rocket (30,000 ft apogee class). The COPV is a seamless spin-formed 6061-T6 aluminum liner overwrapped with a T700-carbon/epoxy filament-wound laminate; a single 1/4" NPT port sits on the forward dome. Service fluid is gaseous nitrogen at 25 C.

Envelope: liner internal diameter 150 mm, cylindrical internal length 300 mm, hemispherical domes.

Load cases.
- LC1 burst: internal pressure 3.0 x MEOP = 900 bar, first-ply-failure (Tsai-Wu) check on the composite wrap.
- LC2 proof: 1.5 x MEOP = 450 bar, held >= 4 minutes (>= 2 x nominal 120 s hold), liner stress check.
- LC3 autofrettage: 1.25 x MEOP cycle, then bleed; liner residual hoop stress must remain compressive.
- LC4 modal: axial hard-mount, first mode floor.

prompt.title: Student-developed COPV (30k ft category nitrogen pressurant)
prompt.brief: Design a filament-wound COPV with 6061-T6 liner, MEOP 300 bar, burst 3x MEOP (900 bar), proof 1.5x MEOP held >= 4 min.

boundary_conditions.mounting: fixed at aft boss, internal pressure on liner inner wall
boundary_conditions.composite_bond: perfectly bonded layered shell at liner interface

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Isothermal 25 C; pressurant temperature transients out of scope.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Internal pressure only; no external crush case in mission profile.
